member.office_address: 2447 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3002

form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
The Office of Congressman Jeff Van Drew (NJ-02)

SUBMISSION GUIDELINES

Appropriations requests must be made no later than COB March 14, 2026.

Send Completed Requests To: NJ02Grants@mail.house.gov

ALL requests MUST include a draft Member letter.

Priority will be given to requests affecting NJ-02.

The request does not guarantee submission by Congressman Van Drew.

For Community Project Funding please contact haddon.antonucci@mail.house.gov with a project description and cost estimate. Submissions are very limited and not guaranteed.

fields.label: Requesting Organization
fields.help_text: The name of the organization making the request.

fields.label: Subcommittee, Agency, and Account
fields.help_text: Identify the relevant House Appropriations Subcommittee, Federal Agency, and specific Budget Account.
fields.draft_value: Subcommittee: Commerce, Justice, Science; Agency: NASA; Account: Science
fields.rationale: This value follows the provided request context specifying the CJS Subcommittee and the Science Mission Directorate account.

fields.label: Request Type: (Program or Language)
fields.help_text: Indicate if this is a request for a specific funding amount (Program) or legislative text (Language).
fields.rationale: The request context explicitly identifies this as a programmatic funding request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Program/Language Title
fields.help_text: The official title of the program or language request.
fields.rationale: This is the specific account name for the $9 billion programmatic request as defined in the reference values.

sections.description: Complete this section if the request is for Bill or Report Language.

fields.label: Language Type (Bill or Report)
fields.help_text: Indicate if the requested language is for the bill text itself or the accompanying committee report.
fields.rationale: This field is not applicable because the request is identified as 'Programmatic' rather than 'Language'.

fields.label: Proposed Language
fields.help_text: Provide the exact text of the language you are requesting.
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic, and the provided reference values state that report and bill language are not requested for this specific submission.

fields.label: Impact on NJ-02
fields.help_text: Explain how this language request specifically benefits or impacts New Jersey's 2nd Congressional District.
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic, making the language request section non-applicable.

sections.description: Complete this section if the request is for Program Funding.

fields.label: Description of Program Request
fields.help_text: A narrative description of the funding request.
fields.draft_value:
* $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to maintain U.S. leadership in space exploration
* Supports peer-reviewed, Decadal Survey priorities across Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences
* Provides budgetary stability to restore workforce capabilities and mitigate delays to critical missions after recent funding fluctuations
* Ensures the U.S. continues to set the international norms for space exploration and scientific discovery in a competitive environment
fields.rationale: The $9 billion ask is the primary programmatic goal defined in the asks_config and reference values. The description uses provided advocacy talking points regarding Decadal Survey priorities, workforce stability, and global leadership.

fields.label: Amount
fields.help_text: The dollar amount requested for the program.
fields.rationale: This is the exact funding ask amount provided in the reference values for the NASA Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Impact on NJ-02
fields.help_text: Explain how this program funding specifically benefits or impacts New Jersey's 2nd Congressional District.
fields.draft_value:
* Supports the broader U.S. aerospace and technology workforce pipeline that provides the talent for New Jersey's innovation economy
* Enhances national capabilities in weather forecasting and environmental monitoring that provide direct value to New Jersey's coastal communities
* Every dollar invested in NASA returns roughly $3 to the economy, driving commercial spinoffs and industrial demand across the region
* Inspires the next generation of STEM professionals in NJ-02 through high-visibility missions and breakthroughs in human knowledge
fields.rationale: While no direct spending was identified in NJ-02 ($0.00), the response draws on the instruction to use broader national importance and economic return data ($3 for every $1 spent) to justify the impact on the district.
